response.justification: Mohammed brings 15+ years of progressively senior full-stack experience with direct matches on the primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) and key secondary requirements (AWS CDK, MongoDB, Docker, Kubernetes). His current role explicitly includes AI agent infrastructure and MCP framework development, which closely aligns with the AI agent development requirement. A strong TDD culture is evidenced not just in practice but through open-source testing tooling and public workshops. The main gaps are the absence of explicit NestJS experience and limited modern Kotlin/Java exposure, neither of which is disqualifying given the overall profile depth.
strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js/React experience across multiple senior and staff-level roles, directly matching the primary stack
Current hands-on AI agent and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq aligns precisely with the AI agent development requirement
Exceptionally strong testing culture: TDD in production, open-source D-language testing libraries, and public workshops on testing and TDD
concerns: No explicit mention of NestJS anywhere in the resume despite it being the primary backend framework
Kotlin/Java exposure is limited and dated — confined to robotics work at Altom (2016–2018), not backend services
No mention of Vitest or Playwright; testing framework specifics listed in skills section are too general to confirm alignment with the required toolchain

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP development (current role)
